title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI
excerpt:
Title: HP Inc. launches Frontier strategic partnership with OpenAI

URL Source: https://openai.com/index/hp-frontier-partnership

Markdown Content:
Enterprise transformation rarely starts all at once. More often, it begins when small teams prove a new way of working is possible. That was the case with HP Inc., which just announced it will scale activation of its [OpenAI Frontier](https://openai.com/business/frontier/) strategic partnership, following a series of successful pilots across different areas.

The strategic partnership extends how HP is deploying frontier capabilities to help its global efforts to enhance customer-facing experiences and accelerate transformation across its operations. Once scaled, the strategic partnership will focus on deploying AI across the organization in areas ranging from customer and partner-facing solutions and experiences, customer telemetry insights and reporting, employee productivity, and software development.

As soon as HP began testing OpenAI Frontier in February 2026, the company started exploring different ways it can use the platform. Early signs of success arrived quickly.

title: UNESCO summit backs ethical AI governance in Latin America and the Caribbean
excerpt:
Title: UNESCO summit backs ethical AI governance in Latin America and the Caribbean | Digital Watch Observatory

URL Source: https://dig.watch/updates/unesco-ai-governance-summit

Published Time: 2026-06-28T14:00:00+02:00

Markdown Content:
_More than 20 delegations joined talks on responsible AI governance in the Dominican Republic._

Representatives from more than 20 Latin American and Caribbean countries have met in Santo Domingo for a regional summit on AI ethics and [governance](https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/third-ministerial-summit-ethics-artificial-intelligence-latin-america-and-caribbean-takes-place).

The Third Ministerial and High-Level Authorities Summit on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence in Latin America and the Caribbean took place on 25 and 26 June in the Dominican Republic. It was organised by [UNESCO](https://dig.watch/actor/united-nations-educational-scientific-and-cultural-organization), the Dominican Republic’s Government Office of Information and Communication Technologies (OGTIC), and CAF – Development Bank of Latin America and the Caribbean.

The summit brought together ministers, senior government officials, multilateral organisations, academics, p

Title: NTT DATA and Cursor partner to accelerate enterprise-grade modernization and AI governance

URL Source: https://www.albawaba.com/business/pr/ntt-data-and-cursor-partner-accelerate-1631509

Published Time: 2026-06-28T05:04:08+0000

Markdown Content:
Press release

Published June 28th, 2026 - 05:04 GMT[Facebook](https://www.albawaba.com/#facebook)[Twitter](https://www.albawaba.com/#twitter)[WhatsApp](https://www.albawaba.com/#whatsapp)

NTT DATA, a global leader in AI, digital business and technology services, today announced a strategic partnership with Cursor, the leading multi-model AI coding platform. Under this initiative, NTT DATA will use Cursor’s advanced AI agents to power the innovation of its global software engineering and delivery models. Cursor will enable NTT DATA to design, build and modernize enterprise systems with greater speed and control, while supporting the governance enterprises require.

title: How a Clean GitHub Repo Tricks Your AI Coding Agent Into Running Malware
excerpt:
Title: How a Clean GitHub Repo Tricks Your AI Coding Agent Into Running Malware

URL Source: https://easternherald.com/2026/06/28/clean-github-repo-ai-coding-agent-malware-prompt-injection/

Published Time: 2026-06-28T13:47:32+00:00

Markdown Content:
[![Image 1: Add as preferred source on Google](https://easternherald.com/wp-content/plugins/add-as-preferred-source/public/images/google-preferred-source-button.png)](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=easternherald.com)

SAN FRANCISCO – The repository contained no malicious code. Not a single line. A researcher from Mozilla’s Zero Day Investigative Network cloned it, opened Claude Code to help set up the project, and watched as the agent encountered a package initialization error and – helpfully – ran a recovery command. That command called an attacker-controlled shell script. The shell script queried a DNS TXT record. The developer’s machine had a reverse shell, and the repository still contained nothing detectable as malware.
